SEARCH
Package fr.sorbonne_universite.ldc.model.leftpanel

Class ThresholdingManager

java.lang.Object
fr.sorbonne_universite.ldc.model.leftpanel.ThresholdingManager
public class ThresholdingManager extends Object
Provides segmentation and thresholding operations on ImagePlus objects.

Supports both manual threshold preview and automatic threshold computation using ImageJ's native setAutoThreshold method with stack histogram support. The final applyThreshold(ImagePlus) method generates a new binary mask without modifying the original image.

See Also:
  • ThresholdingPanel

  • Constructor Details

    • ThresholdingManager

      public ThresholdingManager()

  • Method Details

    • setManualThreshold

      public void setManualThreshold(ij.ImagePlus imp, double min, double max)
      Applies a manual threshold preview to the current image by setting the threshold range with a red LUT overlay.

      This is a visual-only operation and does not generate a binary mask.

      Parameters:
      imp - The image on which the preview must be applied. Does nothing if null.
      min - The minimum pixel value of the threshold range.
      max - The maximum pixel value of the threshold range.

    • setAutoThreshold

      public double[] setAutoThreshold(ij.ImagePlus imp, String method, boolean darkBackground)
      Calculates and applies an automatic threshold (Otsu, Moments, etc.) to the image using the stack histogram (all slices combined) to compute the threshold.

      Delegates to ImageJ's native ImagePlus.setAutoThreshold() with the stack option, ensuring the threshold is computed globally across the entire volume rather than on a single slice. The display range (contrast) is not reset.

      Parameters:
      imp - The image on which the threshold must be computed and previewed. Returns {0, 0} if null.
      method - The name of the auto-threshold algorithm (e.g. "Otsu", "Moments", "Triangle").
      darkBackground - true if the image has a dark background (bright objects), false otherwise.
      Returns:
      An array {min, max} containing the computed threshold bounds.

    • resetThreshold

      public boolean resetThreshold(ij.ImagePlus imp)
      Resets the threshold on the given image, removing any threshold overlay.
      Parameters:
      imp - The image whose threshold must be reset. Does nothing and returns false if null.
      Returns:
      true if the threshold was successfully reset, false otherwise.

    • applyThreshold

      public ij.ImagePlus applyThreshold(ij.ImagePlus originalImp)
      Creates a NEW binary mask image based on the current threshold settings. The original image is left unchanged.
      Parameters:
      originalImp - The source image (will not be modified).
      Returns:
      the NEW binary mask image based on the current threshold settings, or null if an error occurred.
